section_miscategorizations.text: He is currently experiencing some mild abdominal tenderness to light palpation.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Abdominal tenderness is framed as a current exam-type finding rather than a subjective symptom description; it fits better under objective findings, but its presence in HPI is not seriously misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: He reports an EKG and blood tests were performed in the ER, which were normal.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Normal EKG and blood tests are diagnostic results; while patient-reported, they function as objective data and would typically be documented under Findings, though inclusion in HPI is acceptable and not highly misleading.

section_miscategorizations.text: A history of heartburn is present, but the patient denies experiencing it.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This is historical symptom information and current denial, which belongs in the symptom/history narrative rather than the diagnostic synthesis; its placement in Assessment is more stylistic than misleading.
